title: Eva fans and religion
wikitext:
[]: Eva fans and religion Post by palehorseXIII » Mon Oct 10, 2011 11:41 am I read in a thread once that most users here are Atheists or call themselves "Agnostic". I found this interesting as I myself am an Atheist. Is this true, and does this extend to all fans and not just the users of this forum? What is it about the show that may appeal to these faithless individuals? I could see how the religious symbolism could scare off people who actually take these things seriously in real life. Do we come for the sacrilege and stay for the complex story?

[]: Post by ADiesel » Mon Oct 10, 2011 11:48 am Im a fairly serious Christian, I look at it like i look at life, Just because people/characters are not Christian doesn't mean i cant associate with them/it. Just my 00.02

[]: Re: Eva fans and religion Post by Xard » Mon Oct 10, 2011 11:49 am If you seriously think being Eva fan correlates more with one's religious views instead of, say, how much a fucking nerd you are I don't know what to say

[]: Re: Eva fans and religion Post by ADiesel » Mon Oct 10, 2011 11:53 am Xard wrote: If you seriously think being Eva fan correlates more with one's religious views instead of, say, how much a fucking nerd you are I don't know what to say

[]: Post by CJD » Mon Oct 10, 2011 11:54 am I highly doubt it has anything to do with Eva. If there's any correlation it's most likely due to the nature of the internet and forums (Both of which are populated by mostly young adults) rather than anything specific about Eva.

[]: Post by Final Messenger » Mon Oct 10, 2011 11:54 am I watched Eva for sexy robot on monster action (I then stayed for the epic story) it had nothing to do with me being a atheist (hell I knew nothing about the show going into it all the info I had was a commercial saying that Eva was perfection)

[]: Post by Stryker » Mon Oct 10, 2011 12:03 pm I think Anno has said himself that he just used the Christian Crosses and other biblical entities because he thought they were cool.

[]: Re: Eva fans and religion Post by Nice Hat » Mon Oct 10, 2011 12:04 pm I'm a Christian, though I am at an internal war with the institution. I like Evangelion because it's interesting and well constructed, despite the flaws.

[]: Post by SaVaGe » Mon Oct 10, 2011 12:55 pm Does it really matter as long as it's entertaining? Chistian motifs are used all the time. Not always respectfully.

[]: Post by Sachi » Mon Oct 10, 2011 1:01 pm CJD wrote: I highly doubt it has anything to do with Eva. If there's any correlation it's most likely due to the nature of the internet and forums (Both of which are populated by mostly young adults) rather than anything specific about Eva.

[]: Post by Trajan » Mon Oct 10, 2011 1:21 pm I really don't think I belong in any particular religious category. If I had to pick one, I'd say I'm a Taoist. But seriously, religion has nothing to do with liking/disliking Evangelion.

[]: Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Oct 10, 2011 1:26 pm CJD wrote: Both of which are populated by mostly young adults

[]: Post by CJD » Mon Oct 10, 2011 1:36 pm Mr. Tines wrote: Age is another orthogonal quantity.

[]: evangelion&religion Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Mon Oct 10, 2011 1:43 pm I'm not religious in the least,but I still love eva ,as long as no one establishes a religion around eva religious affiliation doesn't matter.

[]: Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Oct 10, 2011 1:44 pm I'm a Christian as well, and tolerate people believeing and practicing other ideas and beliefs. But that also doesn't mean I have to put mine aside when I talk with these people either; or even when I'm watching movies and shows like Evangelion. I find it a fascinating story told in a fascinating way. Heck, I don't even know what religion the creators of the show believe themselves, but it probably didn't influence what they've put in the show. (Unless there's a religion in Japan that focuses on hating otaku. If there is, then I'm pretty sure Anno's a part of it or even had a hand in starting it.)

[]: Post by gatotsu911 » Mon Oct 10, 2011 1:54 pm I'm somewhat of the impression that if there's any external factor that draws people to Eva aside from personal taste, it probably has a lot more to do with personality type and psychological/emotional state than religious beliefs. Speaking personally, I am not a particularly religious person, though I do not consider myself an atheist either. I identify strongly with my religious heritage (Judaism) even though I rarely practice it. I guess I'm what would be considered an agnostic. I really like the way in which Eva approaches the topic of spirituality - and make no mistake, depth (or lack thereof) of religious symbolism aside, spirituality is a topic of great concern to this series and its worldview. Ironically, despite the religious imagery and terminology being constantly thrown in viewers' faces, the actual way in which the show addresses spirituality is fairly subtle and implicit. @FFFFE: Otakuism IS a religion in Japan, and like any good religion, it entails heavy elements of guilt and celibacy. Anno has lapsed and un-lapsed through it multiple times over the course of his life.

[]: Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Oct 10, 2011 1:56 pm CJD wrote: While age is irrelevant by itself, my point was that these are people born in the last 20-30 years, during which time religions have lost a lot of power in the western world.

[]: Post by driftking18594 » Mon Oct 10, 2011 2:05 pm I'd consider myself an atheist-leaning agnostic, and I was a little sceptical when I first heard of EVA. I thought that the people behind it were some sort of Japanese equivalent of Bible-bashers from the Deep South. I am deeply sorry for comparing Anno & Co. to such idiots.

[]: Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Oct 10, 2011 2:16 pm driftking18594 wrote: I'd consider myself an atheist-leaning agnostic, and I was a little sceptical when I first heard of EVA. I thought that the people behind it were some sort of Japanese equivalent of Bible-bashers from the Deep South. I am deeply sorry for comparing Anno & Co. to such idiots.

[]: Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Mon Oct 10, 2011 2:19 pm Mr. Tines wrote: [url]http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=6954[/url]
